channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Toronto Metropolitan University Postdoctoral Research Fellow 2026 in Canada: Earn CAD $65,000 Per Year Plus Benefits
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Researchers passionate about migration, labour market integration, qualitative research, and public policy now have an exciting opportunity to join one of Canada’s newest and most innovative research hubs. Toronto Metropolitan University (TMU) is inviting applications for a Postdoctoral Research Fellow in Qualitative Research and Knowledge Mobilization on Migration and Labour Market Integration in Canada. Hosted […]

The post Toronto Metropolitan University Postdoctoral Research Fellow 2026 in Canada: Earn CAD $65,000 Per Year Plus Benefits appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers passionate about migration, labour market integration, qualitative research, and public policy now have an exciting opportunity to join one of Canada’s newest and most innovative research hubs. Toronto Metropolitan University (TMU) is inviting applications for a Postdoctoral Research Fellow in Qualitative Research and Knowledge Mobilization on Migration and Labour Market Integration in Canada.

Hosted by the BMO Newcomer Workforce Integration Lab within the Global Migration Institute, this two-year, full-time position offers an annual salary of CAD $65,000 plus benefits and the opportunity to contribute to impactful research that informs migration policy, labour market inclusion, and newcomer integration in Canada.

The successful candidate will work closely with internationally recognized migration scholars while collaborating with community organizations, employers, government stakeholders, and policymakers to produce evidence-based research that supports equitable workforce integration.

Applications are open now and must be submitted by 10 July 2026.

About the Toronto Metropolitan University Postdoctoral Fellowship

Toronto Metropolitan University (formerly Ryerson University) is one of Canada’s leading innovation-focused universities, known for combining academic excellence with real-world impact. Through its commitment to equity, diversity, inclusion, and community engagement, the university has built an environment where research directly contributes to solving pressing societal challenges.

This Postdoctoral Research Fellow position is based within the newly established Global Migration Institute, which officially launched in April 2026. The Institute serves as an international centre for research, policy engagement, and public dialogue on migration, labour mobility, integration, and emerging technologies.

The successful fellow will also work within the BMO Newcomer Workforce Integration Lab, an initiative dedicated to improving labour market outcomes for immigrants, international students, temporary residents, employers, and communities across Canada.

About the Global Migration Institute

The Global Migration Institute conducts interdisciplinary research on migration issues affecting Canada and the wider international community.

Its research priorities include:

  • Migration experiences and integration.
  • Labour migration.
  • Immigration policies.
  • Technology and Artificial Intelligence in migration.
  • Public narratives surrounding migration.
  • Migrant inclusion and social participation.
  • Policy innovation.
  • International migration governance.

The Institute brings together researchers, policymakers, employers, community organizations, and international partners to produce practical, evidence-based solutions for migration challenges.

About the BMO Newcomer Workforce Integration Lab

The BMO Newcomer Workforce Integration Lab focuses on improving employment opportunities and workforce integration for newcomers in Canada.

Its research explores:

  • Labour migration.
  • Immigrant employment integration.
  • International students.
  • Temporary residents.
  • Skills recognition.
  • Inclusive workplace practices.
  • Employer engagement.
  • Digital technologies and Artificial Intelligence in recruitment and hiring.

Through collaborative research and knowledge mobilization, the Lab aims to bridge the gap between academic research and practical policy implementation.

Position Overview

The successful Postdoctoral Research Fellow will contribute to multiple externally funded research projects focused on migration and labour market integration.

Working under the supervision of Professor Anna Triandafyllidou, Founding Director of the Global Migration Institute, and Dr. Marshia Akbar, Director of the BMO Newcomer Workforce Integration Lab, the fellow will collaborate with:

  • Researchers.
  • Graduate students.
  • Research assistants.
  • Community organizations.
  • Employers.
  • Government stakeholders.
  • Policy experts.

This role provides an excellent opportunity for early-career researchers to build strong academic and policy experience while contributing to research with real-world impact.

Key Responsibilities

The successful fellow will undertake a wide range of research and knowledge mobilization activities, including:

Research Activities

  • Conduct qualitative research.
  • Perform comprehensive literature reviews.
  • Conduct semi-structured interviews.
  • Facilitate focus groups.
  • Lead stakeholder consultations.
  • Support participant recruitment.
  • Coordinate research participants.

Data Analysis

  • Analyze qualitative datasets.
  • Manage research data using NVivo.
  • Support mixed-methods research projects.
  • Conduct policy scans related to migration and labour market integration.

Knowledge Mobilization

The fellow will contribute to translating research into practical knowledge by:

  • Drafting research reports.
  • Writing academic papers.
  • Developing policy briefs.
  • Preparing presentations.
  • Producing knowledge mobilization resources.

Research Administration

Responsibilities also include:

  • Supporting research ethics applications.
  • Managing research documentation.
  • Coordinating workshops and webinars.
  • Organizing stakeholder meetings and roundtables.
  • Supporting conferences and public engagement events.
  • Assisting with project coordination.
  • Supporting grant development.

Stakeholder Engagement

The fellow will regularly engage with:

  • Academic collaborators.
  • Community organizations.
  • Employers.
  • Government agencies.
  • Policymakers.

The role also involves supporting social media communication and public-facing dissemination of research findings.

Professional Development Opportunities

Beyond research responsibilities, the successful fellow will gain access to numerous career development opportunities, including:

  • Academic mentorship.
  • Publication development.
  • Conference participation.
  • Policy engagement.
  • Collaborative interdisciplinary research.
  • Professional networking.
  • Knowledge mobilization experience.
  • Grant development exposure.

These experiences are designed to strengthen the fellow’s academic profile while preparing them for future research and leadership positions.

Salary and Benefits

The fellowship offers an attractive compensation package that includes:

  • Annual salary of CAD $65,000.
  • Employee benefits.
  • Two-year contract.
  • Full-time employment (36.25 hours per week).
  • In-person work environment.
  • Opportunity to work with internationally recognized migration experts.

The fellowship begins in late September 2026.

Work Location

This is an in-person position based in Toronto, Ontario, Canada.

Remote work is not available for this role.

Successful candidates are expected to relocate to Toronto and work on campus throughout the fellowship period.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must hold a PhD in one of the following disciplines or a closely related social science field:

  • Sociology.
  • Geography.
  • Migration Studies.
  • Public Policy.
  • Political Science.
  • Related disciplines with a strong research focus on migration and labour market integration.

Required Skills and Experience

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong qualitative research experience.
  • Experience conducting interviews and focus groups.
  • Knowledge of migration and labour migration.
  • Understanding of immigrant workforce integration.
  • Experience working on international student or migration issues.
  • Experience using NVivo or similar qualitative analysis software.
  • Excellent academic writing skills.
  • Publication experience.
  • Strong organizational and communication skills.
  • Ability to work in interdisciplinary research teams.
  • Experience conducting community-engaged or policy-oriented research.
  • Ability to manage multiple research projects simultaneously.

Preferred Qualifications

The following qualifications are considered advantageous:

  • Knowledge mobilization experience.
  • Stakeholder engagement experience.
  • Research grant writing.
  • International research experience.
  • Fluency in languages other than English.

Equity, Diversity and Inclusion

Toronto Metropolitan University is committed to creating an inclusive and equitable workplace.

The university particularly encourages applications from historically underrepresented groups, including:

  • First Nations.
  • Métis.
  • Inuit.
  • Indigenous Peoples of North America.
  • Racialized persons.
  • Persons with disabilities.
  • Women.
  • 2SLGBTQ+ individuals.

While applications are welcomed from qualified candidates worldwide, Canadian citizens and permanent residents will receive priority consideration, in accordance with Canadian employment regulations.

Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following:

  1. A Curriculum Vitae (2–4 pages).
  2. A Motivation Letter (maximum 2 pages).
  3. One writing sample (journal article, dissertation chapter, policy brief, or research report).
  4. A list of two potential referees.

The motivation letter should explain:

  • Research experience.
  • Relevant skills.
  • Academic achievements.
  • Suitability for the fellowship.
  • Interest in migration and labour market integration research.

Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted no later than 10 July 2026.

Applicants are encouraged to submit complete application packages well before the deadline to avoid last-minute issues.

Fellowship Summary

Item Details
Institution Toronto Metropolitan University
Department Global Migration Institute
Research Lab BMO Newcomer Workforce Integration Lab
Position Postdoctoral Research Fellow
Country Canada
Duration 2 Years
Salary CAD $65,000 per year
Benefits Included
Work Schedule 36.25 hours per week
Start Date Late September 2026
Location Toronto, Canada (In-person only)
Deadline 10 July 2026

Apply

Apply Here

Final Thoughts

The Toronto Metropolitan University Postdoctoral Research Fellowship 2026 provides an outstanding opportunity for early-career researchers interested in migration, labour market integration, qualitative research, and public policy. With a competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, extensive professional development opportunities, and the chance to influence migration policy and workforce integration strategies in Canada, this fellowship offers an ideal platform for advancing an academic and policy-focused career.

Researchers with strong qualitative research expertise and a passion for migration studies are encouraged to submit their applications before the 10 July 2026 deadline.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Toronto Metropolitan University Postdoctoral Research Fellow 2026 in Canada: Earn CAD $65,000 Per Year Plus Benefits appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded DCU PhD Scholarship 2026 in Ireland: Receive €25,000 Annual Stipend Plus Full Tuition for Four Years
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Are you passionate about public policy, climate action, social justice, housing, governance, or interdisciplinary research? Dublin City University (DCU) is inviting applications for a fully funded PhD scholarship at the School of Law and Government, offering an outstanding opportunity for ambitious researchers from Ireland and around the world to pursue doctoral studies in one of […]

The post Fully Funded DCU PhD Scholarship 2026 in Ireland: Receive €25,000 Annual Stipend Plus Full Tuition for Four Years appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about public policy, climate action, social justice, housing, governance, or interdisciplinary research? Dublin City University (DCU) is inviting applications for a fully funded PhD scholarship at the School of Law and Government, offering an outstanding opportunity for ambitious researchers from Ireland and around the world to pursue doctoral studies in one of Ireland’s leading universities.

The successful candidate will join the SEAI-funded SHINE research project, a four-year interdisciplinary initiative exploring how social housing residents engage with clean energy transitions, climate action, and technological innovation. The scholarship provides a generous annual stipend of €25,000, full tuition coverage (up to the non-EU fee rate), expert supervision, and the opportunity to contribute to impactful research that addresses energy poverty and supports a just transition toward a low-carbon future.

Applications are open now, with the deadline set for 8 July 2026 at 5:00 PM.

About the DCU Fully Funded PhD Scholarship

The School of Law and Government at Dublin City University is offering a fully funded doctoral scholarship for students interested in interdisciplinary research that combines public policy, governance, technology, climate change, and social justice.

The PhD scholarship forms part of the SHINE (Supporting Housing through Intelligent and Networked Energy) research project, funded by the Sustainable Energy Authority of Ireland (SEAI). The project is jointly led by Dr. Valesca Lima from the School of Law and Government and Professor Stephen Daniels from the School of Electronic Engineering.

Over four years, SHINE will investigate how citizen science can help improve understanding of the relationship between social housing, clean energy, climate resilience, and policy development.

The successful PhD researcher will be based in Dublin, Ireland, throughout the duration of the programme and will become part of a collaborative research team working across multiple academic disciplines.

About the SHINE Research Project

The SHINE project aims to address one of today’s most pressing societal challenges—ensuring that the transition to clean energy is both fair and inclusive.

The project recognizes that national climate ambitions cannot be achieved without effectively supporting vulnerable households, particularly those living in social housing.

Its research focuses on developing intelligent and evidence-based solutions that can:

  • Address energy poverty.
  • Improve climate resilience.
  • Support behavioural change.
  • Promote citizen engagement.
  • Encourage a just transition.
  • Integrate emerging technologies into housing policy.

Through citizen science and interdisciplinary collaboration, SHINE seeks to generate knowledge that informs national climate and housing strategies while improving the well-being of communities.

PhD Research Focus

Applicants are encouraged to design their own doctoral research project within the wider SHINE framework.

The university welcomes innovative research proposals that embrace the interdisciplinary nature of the project while addressing challenges related to:

  • Public policy.
  • Housing.
  • Climate governance.
  • Citizen engagement.
  • Technology adoption.
  • Energy systems.
  • Social justice.
  • Policy innovation.

The research should contribute meaningful insights into how governance, technology, and citizen participation can help achieve fair and sustainable climate transitions.

Indicative Research Themes

Potential research topics include:

  • Socio-technical policy and governance.
  • Human-technology interaction.
  • Human-data interaction.
  • Citizen science in housing policy.
  • National housing strategies.
  • Energy literacy.
  • Household behavioural change.
  • Climate action planning.
  • Public policy frameworks.
  • Housing regulations.
  • Just transition governance.
  • Policy innovation.

Applicants are encouraged to develop original research questions that align with these themes while demonstrating creativity and interdisciplinary thinking.

Why You Should Apply

The DCU PhD Scholarship offers much more than financial support.

Successful applicants will benefit from:

  • Full funding for four years.
  • Annual stipend of €25,000.
  • Tuition fees covered up to the non-EU rate.
  • Opportunity to conduct impactful interdisciplinary research.
  • Supervision by experienced academic experts.
  • Membership in a supportive research community.
  • Skills development and professional training.
  • Freedom to shape an independent doctoral research project.
  • Collaboration across public policy and engineering disciplines.
  • Contribution to research that influences climate and housing policy.

Research Environment at Dublin City University

Successful candidates will join one of Ireland’s most dynamic research environments.

DCU provides:

  • Dedicated academic supervision.
  • Collaborative interdisciplinary teams.
  • Opportunities for professional development.
  • Research training.
  • Academic networking.
  • Support in developing independent research careers.

Students are encouraged to pursue innovative ideas while benefiting from guidance from experienced supervisors.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants should possess a strong academic background and an interest in interdisciplinary research.

Essential Qualification

Applicants should hold a Level 8 Bachelor’s Degree in one or more relevant disciplines, including:

  • Political Science.
  • Public Policy.
  • Sociology.
  • Human Geography.
  • Education.
  • Data Science.
  • Environmental Science.
  • Sustainable Systems.
  • Other engineering or environmental fields with a demonstrated interest in social justice.

Desirable Qualification

Applicants with a Level 9 Master’s Degree in a relevant field are particularly encouraged to apply.

Although a master’s degree is desirable, applicants should carefully review the official requirements to determine their eligibility.

Who Can Apply?

Applications are welcomed from:

  • Applicants from Ireland.
  • Applicants from European Union countries.
  • International applicants from around the world.

Successful candidates will be expected to relocate to Dublin and remain based there for the duration of the four-year doctoral programme.

Applicants shortlisted for the position will participate in an online interview during mid-July.

Applicants may also be required to demonstrate English language proficiency where applicable.

Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following:

  1. Curriculum Vitae (maximum three pages).
  2. Motivation Letter (maximum two pages).

Applications should clearly demonstrate:

  • Academic preparation.
  • Research interests.
  • Motivation for pursuing the PhD.
  • Interest in interdisciplinary research.
  • Alignment with the SHINE project objectives.

How to Apply

Completed applications should be submitted via email to the address provided by Dublin City University.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare high-quality application materials that demonstrate their research potential, interdisciplinary interests, and commitment to addressing climate, housing, and social justice challenges.

Informal enquiries regarding the position may be directed to Dr. Valesca Lima.

Application Deadline

Applications close on 8 July 2026 at 5:00 PM.

Late applications may not be considered, so prospective applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare and submit their documents well before the deadline.

Apply: send application to tracymae.ildefonso@dcu.ie 

Scholarship Summary

Item Details
Institution Dublin City University (DCU)
School School of Law and Government
Country Ireland
Funding Fully Funded
Annual Stipend €25,000
Tuition Covered up to the non-EU fee rate
Duration Four years
Project SHINE (SEAI-funded)
Start Date September 2026
Deadline 8 July 2026, 5:00 PM

Final Thoughts

The Fully Funded DCU PhD Scholarship 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for aspiring researchers who want to contribute to climate policy, social justice, energy transitions, and public governance. By joining the SHINE research project, doctoral candidates will work on meaningful interdisciplinary research with real-world policy implications while receiving comprehensive financial support throughout their studies.

If you are passionate about addressing energy poverty, promoting sustainable housing policies, and shaping equitable climate solutions, this fully funded PhD programme at Dublin City University could be the ideal next step in your academic and professional journey. Be sure to submit your application before the 8 July 2026, 5:00 PM deadline.

For more information, visit the official call for applications

Discover more global scholarship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Fully Funded DCU PhD Scholarship 2026 in Ireland: Receive €25,000 Annual Stipend Plus Full Tuition for Four Years appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Canberra Research Scholarships 2026–2027: Fully Funded PhD and Higher Degree Research Opportunities in Australia
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The University of Canberra in Australia has announced a wide range of prestigious research scholarships for domestic and international students seeking to pursue Higher Degree by Research (HDR) programs, including Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) and Master’s by Research studies. These scholarships are designed to support outstanding researchers across diverse disciplines, including business, law, public health, […]

The post University of Canberra Research Scholarships 2026–2027: Fully Funded PhD and Higher Degree Research Opportunities in Australia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Canberra in Australia has announced a wide range of prestigious research scholarships for domestic and international students seeking to pursue Higher Degree by Research (HDR) programs, including Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) and Master’s by Research studies.

These scholarships are designed to support outstanding researchers across diverse disciplines, including business, law, public health, democracy studies, artificial intelligence, climate change, data science, Indigenous research, and many other academic fields. Successful applicants may receive generous stipends, tuition fee coverage, research funding, relocation support, health insurance, and additional benefits depending on the scholarship.

If you are planning to undertake advanced research studies in Australia, the University of Canberra offers multiple funding opportunities that can significantly reduce the financial burden associated with postgraduate research education.

About the University of Canberra Research Scholarships

The University of Canberra provides several faculty-managed and externally funded scholarships aimed at attracting exceptional research talent from Australia and around the world. These scholarships support students who demonstrate strong academic achievement, research potential, and commitment to contributing to knowledge creation in their chosen fields.

The available scholarships cover a variety of research areas and cater to different categories of applicants, including Indigenous Australians, domestic students, international students, and candidates pursuing specialized research projects.

Available University of Canberra Research Scholarships

1. Indigenous Australian Higher Degree by Research Stipend Scholarship

This scholarship supports Indigenous Australian students pursuing a Higher Degree by Research at the University of Canberra.

Benefits

  • AUD $36,225 per annum tax-free stipend (2025 rate)
  • Funding for up to:
    • Four years for doctoral programs
    • Two years for master’s research programs
  • Additional research funding worth AUD $10,000
  • Funding can be used for project-related expenses

Eligibility

Applicants must:

  • Be enrolled or seeking enrollment in a Higher Degree by Research program at the University of Canberra
  • Meet university admission requirements
  • Identify as an Indigenous Australian
  • Study full-time

2. W J Weeden Postgraduate Research Scholarship

Funded by the family of the late W J Weeden, this scholarship supports outstanding doctoral researchers.

Benefits

  • AUD $36,225 per annum tax-free stipend
  • Annual indexation
  • Overseas Student Health Cover reimbursement for eligible international students
  • Research Training Program Fee Offset Scholarship for international recipients
  • Tuition fee coverage for international students

Duration

  • 3.5 years

Eligibility

Applicants must:

  • Hold a Bachelor degree with First Class Honours or equivalent
  • Be enrolled or intending to enroll in a Doctor of Philosophy program
  • Be either:
    • Australian citizens
    • Australian permanent residents
    • New Zealand citizens
    • International students

3. Australian Research Training Program Fees Offset Scholarship

This scholarship assists eligible Higher Degree by Research students with tuition fees.

Benefits

  • Tuition fee support
  • Research Training Program Fee Offset coverage

Eligibility

Applicants must:

  • Be Australian citizens, permanent residents, or New Zealand citizens
  • Apply for or currently undertake:
    • PhD
    • Professional Doctorate
    • Master’s by Research

International candidates who receive eligible stipend scholarships may also be considered automatically.

4. Organisational Creativity and Artificial Intelligence Doctor of Philosophy Stipend Scholarship

Offered by the Faculty of Business, Government and Law, this scholarship focuses on understanding how technology affects creativity, curiosity, and empowerment among consumers and organizations.

Benefits

  • AUD $40,000 annual stipend
  • Indexed annually

Duration

  • 3.5 years
  • Commencement: Semester 1, 2027

Eligibility

Applicants must:

  • Be Australian citizens or permanent residents
  • Meet Doctor of Philosophy admission requirements
  • Meet English language requirements
  • Not previously hold a PhD
  • Enroll full-time
  • Commence in the year of the offer

Application Deadline

15 August 2026

Interested applicants must initially contact the CanBe Lab Director with:

  • Curriculum Vitae
  • Academic results
  • One-page research proposal

5. Deliberative Democracy Doctor of Philosophy Scholarship

The University of Canberra’s Centre for Deliberative Democracy is offering a fully funded doctoral scholarship for research related to democratic participation, democratic resilience, political innovation, environmental politics, inclusion, gender studies, and digital democracy.

Benefits

  • AUD $40,000 annual stipend
  • Annual indexation
  • Relocation allowance for international students

Duration

  • 3.5 years

Research Areas

Potential topics include:

  • Democratic resilience
  • Deliberative reasoning
  • Political participation
  • Artificial intelligence and digital politics
  • Environmental governance
  • Identity and inclusion
  • Feminist political research
  • Democratic innovations

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  1. Research proposal (up to 3 pages)
  2. Curriculum Vitae
  3. Academic transcripts
  4. Writing sample
  5. Letter of recommendation
  6. Two referee contacts

Important Dates

  • Application Deadline: 10 August 2026
  • Interviews: 14–18 September 2026
  • Decision Notification: 21 September 2026
  • Program Start: February 2027

Eligibility

Applicants must possess:

  • Bachelor degree with First Class or Upper Second Class Honours, or
  • Master’s degree with substantial research component, or
  • Equivalent qualification recognized by the University

Both domestic and international applicants are eligible.

6. Commonwealth Scientific and Industrial Research Organisation Industry Doctor of Philosophy Scholarship Program

The Commonwealth Scientific and Industrial Research Organisation (CSIRO) Industry Doctor of Philosophy Program connects students with industry partners and Australia’s leading science agency to conduct impact-driven research.

Benefits

  • AUD $45,000 annual scholarship package
  • Up to AUD $13,000 annually for project expenses and development

Duration

  • Four years

Eligibility

Applicants must:

  • Be Australian citizens, permanent residents, or New Zealand citizens
  • Meet university admission requirements
  • Meet English language requirements
  • Enroll full-time
  • Not have previously completed a PhD

7. Data61 Doctor of Philosophy Scholarship

Data61 supports outstanding doctoral researchers in data science and related fields.

Benefits

  • Research Training Program base stipend
  • Additional top-up scholarship of AUD $10,000 annually

Duration

  • 3.5 years

Eligibility

  • Domestic students
  • International students residing in Australia
  • Overseas international students holding study visas

8. Geospatial Modelling of Dengue Co-Funded Stipend Doctor of Philosophy Scholarship

This scholarship supports research investigating the impact of climate change on dengue transmission in the Asia-Pacific region.

Benefits

  • AUD $35,000 annual stipend

Duration

  • Three years

Research Focus

  • Public health
  • Climate change
  • Disease modelling
  • Geospatial analysis
  • Dengue transmission

9. Elite Editing Thesis Write-up Scholarship

Designed to support research students during the final stages of thesis completion.

Benefits

For students without another scholarship:

  • AUD $6,000 total tax-free stipend

For students with another scholarship:

  • AUD $3,600 total tax-free stipend

Eligibility

  • Full-time Master’s or PhD students
  • Australian and New Zealand universities
  • Domestic and international students

Application Period

  • Opens: 1 January annually
  • Closes: 30 June annually

10. Fulbright Postgraduate Scholarships

These prestigious scholarships support Australian postgraduate students undertaking research or study in the United States.

Benefits

May include:

  • Monthly living stipend
  • Tuition assistance
  • Return airfare
  • Establishment allowance
  • Dependent allowance
  • Health and accident insurance

Duration

  • Four to ten months for research placements

Application Period

  • January to July annually

11. Fulbright Indigenous Scholarships

These scholarships support Indigenous scholars conducting research programs in the United States.

Benefits

  • Monthly stipend
  • Return airfare
  • Establishment allowance
  • Dependent allowance
  • Health insurance

Duration

  • Up to one academic year

12. Ramsay Centre Postgraduate Scholarships

The Ramsay Centre supports high-achieving Australians pursuing postgraduate studies at leading universities worldwide.

Benefits

  • Funding support for overseas postgraduate study
  • Access to world-class academic institutions

Application Timeline

  • Opens: Early February annually
  • Closes: Late March annually

Eligibility

  • Australian citizens
  • Australian permanent residents
  • Applicants admitted to a postgraduate degree at a leading international university

Why These Scholarships Stand Out

The University of Canberra and its partner organizations offer some of Australia’s most competitive research scholarships. These opportunities provide:

  • Fully funded doctoral education
  • Generous annual stipends
  • Tuition fee coverage
  • Research project funding
  • Health insurance support
  • International relocation assistance
  • Access to world-class research centers
  • Collaboration with industry and government partners
  • Opportunities for global research engagement

For aspiring researchers seeking advanced study opportunities in Australia, these scholarships provide a pathway to academic excellence, impactful research, and professional growth.

Application Deadlines

Key upcoming deadlines include:

  • Deliberative Democracy PhD Scholarship – 10 August 2026
  • Organisational Creativity and Artificial Intelligence PhD Scholarship – 15 August 2026
  • Elite Editing Thesis Write-up Scholarship – 30 June annually
  • Fulbright Scholarships – Applications open January to July annually
  • Ramsay Centre Postgraduate Scholarships – Late March annually

Prospective applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their applications early and ensure all required documents are submitted before the relevant deadlines.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Scholarhsip Opportunities on OFY here.

The post University of Canberra Research Scholarships 2026–2027: Fully Funded PhD and Higher Degree Research Opportunities in Australia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: New Commons Incubator for Indigenous Languages and Cultures 2026: Fully Supported Six-Month Program for Indigenous-Led Teams Developing Data Commons Projects
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The New Commons Incubator for Indigenous Languages and Cultures 2026 is now accepting applications from Indigenous-led teams seeking to transform innovative ideas into fundable and actionable data commons initiatives. Designed to strengthen Indigenous languages, cultures, and knowledge systems in the age of Artificial Intelligence (AI), this six-month capacity-building program provides participants with the tools, mentorship, […]

The post New Commons Incubator for Indigenous Languages and Cultures 2026: Fully Supported Six-Month Program for Indigenous-Led Teams Developing Data Commons Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The New Commons Incubator for Indigenous Languages and Cultures 2026 is now accepting applications from Indigenous-led teams seeking to transform innovative ideas into fundable and actionable data commons initiatives. Designed to strengthen Indigenous languages, cultures, and knowledge systems in the age of Artificial Intelligence (AI), this six-month capacity-building program provides participants with the tools, mentorship, networks, and technical support needed to develop viable data commons proposals.

Applications are open until 14 August 2026.

The initiative is led by the Open Data Policy Lab at The GovLab in partnership with Microsoft and New Commons Initiatives. It aims to support Indigenous communities in creating responsible and community-governed data ecosystems that can preserve, revitalize, and sustainably manage Indigenous languages, cultural heritage, and knowledge.

About the New Commons Incubator

The New Commons Incubator is a structured capacity-building program focused on helping participants transform early-stage ideas into fundable projects. Through training, mentorship, peer learning, technical guidance, and networking opportunities, the incubator supports teams in building strong foundations for impactful data commons initiatives.

For the 2026 cohort, the program specifically focuses on Indigenous Languages and Cultures, recognizing the urgent need to safeguard and strengthen Indigenous knowledge systems in an increasingly digital and AI-driven world.

Participants will receive support in developing proposals that can attract funding and partnerships while ensuring responsible stewardship of Indigenous data and cultural assets.

What Is a Data Commons?

A data commons is a collaboratively governed data ecosystem that pools and manages access to high-quality datasets in a responsible manner.

These data ecosystems can be used to support:

  • Responsible Artificial Intelligence development
  • Climate adaptation initiatives
  • Biodiversity conservation
  • Public health programs
  • Pandemic preparedness efforts
  • Sustainable agriculture
  • Scientific research
  • Disaster response and resilience
  • Preservation of Indigenous knowledge systems
  • Revitalization of Indigenous languages
  • Protection of cultural heritage
  • Development of Indigenous data economies

By bringing together diverse datasets under shared governance structures, data commons create opportunities for communities to generate social, cultural, scientific, and economic value while maintaining control over how their data is used.

Focus on Indigenous Languages and Cultures

The 2026 Incubator is dedicated to strengthening the pipeline of Indigenous-led data commons initiatives focused on language preservation, cultural revitalization, and responsible knowledge stewardship.

As Artificial Intelligence technologies increasingly rely on large datasets, many Indigenous languages and cultural resources remain underrepresented. This incubator seeks to address that gap by empowering Indigenous communities to shape how their data is collected, governed, preserved, and utilized.

The program supports initiatives that place Indigenous leadership, community priorities, and ethical governance at the center of data management and AI innovation.

Benefits of Participating in the Program

Selected teams will gain access to a comprehensive support system designed to accelerate the development of their projects.

Benefits include:

1. Six Months of Capacity Building

Participants will engage in an intensive learning journey that helps transform concepts into viable project proposals.

2. Expert Training

The program offers structured training sessions covering the design, governance, implementation, and sustainability of data commons initiatives.

3. Mentorship Opportunities

Participants will receive guidance from experts in data governance, AI, Indigenous data stewardship, and community-led innovation.

4. Technical Guidance

Teams will receive support in addressing technical challenges and designing practical frameworks for implementation.

5. Peer Learning Environment

The incubator brings together Indigenous-led teams from diverse backgrounds, creating opportunities for collaboration and knowledge exchange.

6. Networking Opportunities

Participants can connect with:

  • Funders
  • Potential partners
  • Data governance experts
  • Researchers
  • Policy leaders
  • Technology specialists

7. Support for Fundable Proposals

One of the program’s primary objectives is to help teams develop proposals that are attractive to funding organizations and strategic partners.

8. In-Person Kickoff During United Nations General Assembly Week

The first cohort will begin with an in-person kickoff event held during United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) Week in New York City, offering participants valuable exposure and networking opportunities.

Key Areas of Interest

The incubator welcomes projects that contribute to:

  • Indigenous language preservation
  • Language revitalization initiatives
  • Cultural heritage protection
  • Indigenous knowledge stewardship
  • Ethical data governance
  • Community-controlled AI development
  • Indigenous data sovereignty
  • Digital preservation projects
  • Community-driven research initiatives
  • Sustainable Indigenous data ecosystems

Program Partners

The New Commons Incubator is delivered through collaboration among leading organizations committed to responsible data governance and public-interest innovation.

Partners include:

  • Open Data Policy Lab
  • The GovLab
  • Microsoft
  • New Commons Initiatives

Together, these organizations support the development of innovative data commons projects that benefit communities while promoting ethical and responsible use of data.

Why Data Commons Matter in the AI Era

Artificial Intelligence systems increasingly depend on large and diverse datasets. Without deliberate efforts to include Indigenous voices and governance structures, many Indigenous languages and cultural assets risk exclusion from future technological developments.

Data commons offer a pathway for communities to:

  • Maintain control over their data
  • Establish community governance mechanisms
  • Promote ethical AI development
  • Protect cultural heritage
  • Preserve linguistic diversity
  • Create opportunities for future generations

By supporting Indigenous-led initiatives, the New Commons Incubator contributes to a more inclusive and equitable digital future.

Application Deadline

Applications close on 14 August 2026.

Interested Indigenous-led teams are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline and begin the journey toward building impactful data commons initiatives that support Indigenous languages, cultures, and knowledge systems.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post New Commons Incubator for Indigenous Languages and Cultures 2026: Fully Supported Six-Month Program for Indigenous-Led Teams Developing Data Commons Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Anna Freud BeeWell Doctor of Philosophy Studentship in Evidence Based Child and Adolescent Mental Health 2026
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Anna Freud is inviting applications for the BeeWell Doctor of Philosophy Studentship in Evidence Based Child and Adolescent Mental Health, a funded research opportunity designed for a motivated researcher who wants to contribute to meaningful change in the mental health and wellbeing of children and young people. This full-time, three-year funded studentship offers the selected […]

The post Anna Freud BeeWell Doctor of Philosophy Studentship in Evidence Based Child and Adolescent Mental Health 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Anna Freud is inviting applications for the BeeWell Doctor of Philosophy Studentship in Evidence Based Child and Adolescent Mental Health, a funded research opportunity designed for a motivated researcher who wants to contribute to meaningful change in the mental health and wellbeing of children and young people. This full-time, three-year funded studentship offers the selected candidate the chance to undertake doctoral research connected to the BeeWell programme while working with Anna Freud, a leading mental health charity focused on supporting children, young people, and their families.

The opportunity is especially important for applicants who are passionate about child and adolescent mental health, wellbeing, public health, psychology, social sciences, quantitative research, and evidence-based practice. Through this studentship, the successful candidate will investigate the drivers of young people’s mental health and wellbeing, using large-scale datasets and advanced quantitative analysis to generate insights that can inform real-world action.

Anna Freud’s mission is to close the gap in wellbeing and mental health by advancing, translating, delivering, and sharing the best science and practice with everyone who affects the lives of children, young people, and families. This studentship supports that mission by helping build stronger evidence around what influences young people’s mental health, wellbeing, and health behaviours.

About the BeeWell Doctor of Philosophy Studentship

The BeeWell Doctor of Philosophy Studentship is a funded doctoral research opportunity in Evidence Based Child and Adolescent Mental Health. The successful applicant will contribute to research linked to the BeeWell programme, which focuses on understanding and improving the wellbeing of young people.

This studentship combines doctoral study with applied research. The selected candidate will not only work toward a Doctor of Philosophy qualification through University College London but will also contribute to a real-world programme that engages with schools, project partners, and multidisciplinary research teams.

The studentship is structured as a full-time, fixed-term opportunity lasting three years. It includes an annual stipend paid termly by Anna Freud, as well as tuition fees covered at the Home United Kingdom rate.

Funding and Stipend Details

The selected candidate will receive financial support through an annual stipend, paid termly by Anna Freud. Tuition fees will also be covered at the Home United Kingdom rate.

The stipend is structured as follows:

  1. Year 1: £24,174
  2. Year 2: £24,906
  3. Year 3: £25,657

This funding structure makes the studentship a strong opportunity for applicants who want to pursue doctoral research while contributing to applied mental health research in a respected organisation.

Location and Working Arrangement

The studentship is based in London or Manchester, with attendance in person at University College London courses required. This means applicants should be prepared to attend required doctoral courses in London as part of their programme.

The opportunity also includes hybrid working. The successful candidate may work from either of the following Anna Freud sites:

  1. Anna Freud London site: 4-8 Rodney Street, London N1 9JH
  2. Northern Hub: Jactin House, 24 Hood Street, Manchester M4 6WX

This hybrid arrangement provides flexibility while still requiring in-person engagement with University College London and the wider doctoral programme.

Main Research Focus

The studentship focuses on research into the factors that shape young people’s mental health and wellbeing. Through the BeeWell programme, the successful candidate will use large-scale datasets to examine what drives wellbeing, mental health outcomes, and health behaviours among young people.

This research is intended to produce evidence-based insights that can support positive change for children and young people. The work will be collaborative, data-driven, and connected to real-world practice.

The successful candidate will be expected to develop and deliver a Doctor of Philosophy research project aligned with the key research themes of the BeeWell programme. This includes designing research questions, choosing appropriate methodology, analysing data, interpreting findings, and communicating research outcomes clearly.

Responsibilities of the Selected Candidate

The successful applicant will be expected to take on a wide range of research and programme-related responsibilities. These responsibilities include:

  1. Conducting research using large-scale datasets to investigate the drivers of young people’s mental health, wellbeing, and health behaviours.
  2. Developing and delivering a Doctor of Philosophy research project aligned with the major research themes of the BeeWell programme.
  3. Analysing and interpreting complex quantitative data to produce high-quality research outputs and evidence-based insights.
  4. Contributing to the wider delivery of the BeeWell programme, including supporting planned analyses and engagement activities with schools and project partners.
  5. Working collaboratively with a multidisciplinary research team while undertaking doctoral studies through University College London.
  6. Following Anna Freud’s policies, values, and safeguarding responsibilities throughout the studentship.

This role requires someone who can work independently on doctoral research while also contributing to a larger collaborative research programme.

Academic and Research Background Required

Anna Freud is looking for a motivated and analytically minded researcher with a strong interest in children and young people’s mental health and wellbeing. The ideal candidate should have a solid academic background, experience with quantitative research methods, and a clear desire to carry out research that can improve outcomes for young people.

Applicants are expected to bring:

  1. An undergraduate degree in Psychology or a related discipline, such as social sciences, public health, mental health, or another research-focused subject.
  2. A Master’s degree, or equivalent experience working within a research environment.
  3. A demonstrable interest in children and young people’s mental health, wellbeing, or related areas of research.
  4. Experience with quantitative research methods, data analysis, and complex datasets.
  5. The ability to develop and communicate a clear research proposal, including research questions, methodology, analysis plans, and the potential impact of the findings.

The opportunity is suitable for applicants who are comfortable with data-driven research and who want to use evidence to support better mental health and wellbeing outcomes for children and young people.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit key materials as part of the application process. These documents are important because they help demonstrate the applicant’s academic preparation, research interests, methodological ability, and alignment with the BeeWell programme.

Applicants will be required to submit:

  1. An Expression of Interest explaining how their skills, knowledge, and experience match the requirements of the Doctor of Philosophy studentship.
  2. A Research Proposal addressing the key research themes of the BeeWell project.

The Research Proposal should include:

  1. A brief introduction
  2. Research question or research questions
  3. Proposed methodology
  4. Proposed analysis
  5. Potential implications of the findings

Applicants are encouraged to review the BeeWell programme and its existing research before preparing their application and research proposal. This will help applicants design a stronger proposal that connects clearly with the programme’s priorities and research direction.

Important Application Dates

The closing date for applications is midday, 12:00 PM, Monday, 29 June 2026.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply promptly because the advert may close early if Anna Freud receives a high volume of applications.

Shortlisted applicants will be notified no later than Wednesday, 1 July 2026. Interviews are scheduled to take place on Wednesday, 8 July 2026, and Monday, 13 July 2026.

Additional Conditions for Applicants

Applicants must be eligible for entry to the University College London Faculty of Brain Sciences Doctor of Philosophy programme from September 2026. Applicants must also qualify for tuition fees at the Home United Kingdom rate.

The successful candidate will be asked to provide evidence of their Right to Work in the United Kingdom after receiving a job offer. Anna Freud does not hold a sponsor licence and cannot provide visa sponsorship for this opportunity.

Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion Commitment

Anna Freud states that equity, diversity, and inclusion are central to its recruitment practices. The organisation considers candidates who meet the job criteria regardless of ethnic origin, religion or belief, gender, sexual orientation, disability, age, socioeconomic background, caring responsibilities, or care experience.

Applications are submitted anonymously and assessed through a fair evaluation process using the criteria set out in the job profile. Anna Freud also identifies as a Disability Confident employer, meaning disabled candidates who meet the criteria are guaranteed an interview.

The organisation also asks candidates to share diversity information to help identify, address, and prevent bias across the employee lifecycle. Anna Freud believes that a diverse workforce strengthens its ability to support mental health and wellbeing and to better meet the needs of children, young people, and families.

Staff Benefits and Support

The studentship is also connected to Anna Freud’s broader staff support and wellbeing offer. Benefits include access to Perkbox, an all-in-one rewards and recognition platform, as well as wellbeing support such as finishing early on Fridays and free counselling through the Employee Assistance Programme.

Anna Freud also has staff-led Diversity Networks, which provide opportunities for learning, connection, and impact. These networks support an inclusive workplace culture and help staff engage meaningfully with equity, diversity, and inclusion.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should prepare their Expression of Interest and Research Proposal carefully, ensuring that both documents clearly show their academic background, research experience, quantitative skills, and interest in children and young people’s mental health.

Applicants should also review the BeeWell programme and its existing research before submitting their application. A strong application should demonstrate clear alignment with the programme’s themes, show a thoughtful understanding of the research area, and present a realistic plan for doctoral research.

The deadline to apply is 12:00 PM on Monday, 29 June 2026.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Anna Freud BeeWell Doctor of Philosophy Studentship in Evidence Based Child and Adolescent Mental Health 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNSW Global Student Fellowship 2026: Receive Up to AU$8,500 to Launch Your Human Rights Career Across the Asia-Pacific Region
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Students passionate about human rights, international development, social justice, humanitarian action, and global impact now have an exciting opportunity to gain real-world experience through the Australian Human Rights Institute’s Global Student Fellowship Program at the University of New South Wales (UNSW). Applications are officially open from 22 June to 19 July for this highly regarded […]

The post UNSW Global Student Fellowship 2026: Receive Up to AU$8,500 to Launch Your Human Rights Career Across the Asia-Pacific Region appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Students passionate about human rights, international development, social justice, humanitarian action, and global impact now have an exciting opportunity to gain real-world experience through the Australian Human Rights Institute’s Global Student Fellowship Program at the University of New South Wales (UNSW).

Applications are officially open from 22 June to 19 July for this highly regarded fellowship program, which provides selected UNSW students with financial support and hands-on professional experience through placements with some of the world’s leading human rights and development organizations.

The fellowship serves as a powerful career-launching platform, allowing students to work directly with internationally recognized organizations while contributing to meaningful projects that address some of the world’s most pressing social, humanitarian, and development challenges.

What Is the Global Student Fellowship Program?

The Global Student Fellowship Program is an initiative of the Australian Human Rights Institute that supports students in undertaking professional placements with leading human rights and international development organizations located in Australia and across the Asia-Pacific region.

The program is designed to bridge the gap between academic learning and practical experience by enabling students to apply their knowledge in real-world settings. Fellows gain exposure to professional environments where they can contribute to projects focused on human rights advocacy, development policy, humanitarian engineering, migration, refugee protection, community development, and social justice.

By participating in the fellowship, students not only strengthen their professional skills but also expand their networks and gain valuable international experience that can significantly enhance future career opportunities.

Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows receive a range of benefits that support both their professional growth and placement experience.

Financial Support

Global Student Fellows receive:

  • Awards of up to AU$8,500
  • Financial assistance to support placement-related expenses
  • Opportunities to undertake international placements that may otherwise be financially inaccessible

Professional Experience

Participants gain:

  • Direct experience working with globally respected organizations
  • Exposure to international development and human rights initiatives
  • Opportunities to collaborate with experts and practitioners in the field
  • Enhanced practical knowledge and professional competencies

Career Development

The fellowship helps students:

  • Build competitive resumes
  • Develop professional networks
  • Gain experience valued by international organizations
  • Explore future career pathways in human rights and development sectors
  • Increase employability within international institutions, NGOs, government agencies, and multilateral organizations

International Exposure

Placements are available in multiple locations throughout the Asia-Pacific region, providing students with opportunities to:

  • Experience different cultural environments
  • Learn from diverse communities
  • Understand regional development challenges
  • Contribute to impactful international projects

Fellowship Placement Locations

The six-week fellowship placements take place in a range of locations across Australia and the Asia-Pacific region.

Examples of host countries include:

  • Sri Lanka
  • Thailand
  • Timor-Leste
  • Vietnam
  • Cambodia
  • Australia

The availability of specific locations may vary depending on host organization partnerships and annual placement opportunities.

Partner Organizations

One of the most attractive aspects of the Global Student Fellowship Program is the opportunity to work alongside leading international organizations committed to advancing human rights and sustainable development.

Examples of fellowship hosts include:

  1. World Food Programme (WFP)
  2. Human Rights Watch
  3. Engineers Without Borders Australia
  4. International Organization for Migration (IOM)
  5. Jubilee Australia
  6. Additional partner organizations within the human rights and development sectors

These organizations provide fellows with meaningful projects and mentorship opportunities that help cultivate future leaders in social impact and humanitarian work.

Why Students Should Apply

The Global Student Fellowship offers more than just financial support. It provides a transformative professional experience that can shape long-term career trajectories.

Key reasons to apply include:

1. Gain Practical Human Rights Experience

Students can move beyond classroom learning and actively contribute to projects that address real-world human rights and development challenges.

2. Work With Leading Organizations

Few student opportunities offer direct engagement with internationally recognized organizations operating at the forefront of human rights advocacy and development work.

3. Build International Networks

Fellows develop valuable professional relationships with experts, practitioners, policymakers, and community leaders.

4. Strengthen Career Prospects

The fellowship provides a strong foundation for careers in:

  • Human rights law
  • International development
  • Humanitarian assistance
  • Social policy
  • Global governance
  • Refugee and migration services
  • Community development
  • Sustainable development

5. Receive Financial Support

The funding provided helps remove financial barriers and enables students to focus on gaining meaningful professional experience.

Success Stories from Former Fellows

The impact of the fellowship can be seen through the achievements of past participants who have gone on to pursue influential careers in human rights and development.

Advaita – Engineers Without Borders, Cambodia

Advaita participated in a fellowship placement with Engineers Without Borders in Cambodia during 2025/26. The experience allowed her to connect her academic studies in humanitarian engineering with practical development work and community engagement.

Alina – International Organization for Migration, Thailand

After completing her fellowship in Thailand, Alina successfully joined the World Bank’s Young Professionals Program. She credits the fellowship with helping her stand out during recruitment and preparing her for work focused on integrating human rights principles into international development operations.

Carlos – Human Rights Watch, Sydney

Carlos conducted research on refugee rights and international refugee law through his fellowship with Human Rights Watch. The experience strengthened his commitment to pursuing a career in the human rights sector.

Madeline – Jubilee Australia, Sydney

Madeline leveraged her fellowship experience to prepare for a role as a Judicial Affairs Officer with the United Nations Mission in Kosovo. She highlighted the fellowship’s role in building confidence, broadening her perspective, and expanding her understanding of the many pathways available within human rights work.

Program Duration

The fellowship consists of:

  • Six-week professional placements
  • Structured engagement with host organizations
  • Practical project-based work
  • Mentorship and professional development opportunities

How the Fellowship Supports Future Human Rights Leaders

The Australian Human Rights Institute created the Global Student Fellowship Program to empower the next generation of human rights defenders and development professionals.

The initiative recognizes that meaningful change requires talented and passionate individuals equipped with practical experience, global perspectives, and professional networks. Through financial support and placement opportunities, the fellowship helps students build the skills and confidence needed to contribute effectively to social justice and human rights initiatives worldwide.

How to Apply

Click here for more and to apply.

Important Dates

  • Applications Open: 22 June
  • Applications Close: 19 July

Applicants are encouraged to prepare their materials early and review all eligibility requirements and application instructions before submitting.

Final Thoughts

The UNSW Global Student Fellowship Program represents an exceptional opportunity for students seeking to transform their passion for human rights and development into meaningful professional experience. With funding of up to AU$8,500, access to prestigious international organizations, and placements across Australia and the Asia-Pacific region, the fellowship provides a unique platform for students to develop practical skills, expand their professional networks, and begin building impactful careers dedicated to social change.

Students interested in human rights, international development, humanitarian action, and global justice should strongly consider applying before the 19 July deadline.

Click here for more global fellowship opportunities

The post UNSW Global Student Fellowship 2026: Receive Up to AU$8,500 to Launch Your Human Rights Career Across the Asia-Pacific Region appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: IHE Delft Blue Scholarships 2026–2028: Up to 50% Tuition Fee Reduction for International Master’s Students in Water and Sustainable Development
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
International students seeking advanced education in water management, sustainability, governance, and environmental development now have an excellent opportunity to reduce the financial burden of studying abroad through the IHE Delft Blue Scholarships 2026–2028. Offered by IHE Delft Institute for Water Education in the Netherlands, these partial scholarships are designed to support academically qualified self-paying students […]

The post IHE Delft Blue Scholarships 2026–2028: Up to 50% Tuition Fee Reduction for International Master’s Students in Water and Sustainable Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
International students seeking advanced education in water management, sustainability, governance, and environmental development now have an excellent opportunity to reduce the financial burden of studying abroad through the IHE Delft Blue Scholarships 2026–2028.

Offered by IHE Delft Institute for Water Education in the Netherlands, these partial scholarships are designed to support academically qualified self-paying students who have been admitted to either the MSc Programme in Water and Sustainable Development or the Research MSc Programme in Water and Sustainable Development.

The scholarship scheme includes three funding opportunities:

  • Delft Blue Water Leader Scholarship
  • Delft Blue Top Water Scientist Scholarship
  • Delft Blue Scholarship

Together, these scholarships are expected to support approximately 30 students during the 2026–2028 academic cycle.

About the IHE Delft Blue Scholarships

The IHE Delft Blue Scholarships are partial tuition fee reduction awards created to help talented students who demonstrate academic excellence and financial need pursue postgraduate education in the field of water and sustainable development.

The scholarships are available to students who have successfully secured academic admission into one of IHE Delft’s master’s programmes and can demonstrate that they require financial assistance to pursue their studies.

The scholarships do not provide full funding. Successful applicants must show evidence that they can cover the remaining tuition, living expenses, insurance, visa, and accommodation costs.

Delft Blue Water Leader Scholarship

Scholarship Value

The Delft Blue Water Leader Scholarship provides:

  • 50% reduction of tuition fees

This scholarship is available for students admitted to the MSc Programme in Water and Sustainable Development.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 1 July 2026

Who Is This Scholarship For?

The scholarship targets:

  • Self-paying students
  • Academically admitted applicants
  • Candidates with excellent academic performance
  • Students with financial need

Selection Criteria

Applicants may be assessed based on a combination of the following:

  1. Excellent academic background
    • High Bachelor’s or Master’s GPA
    • Relevant academic qualifications
  2. Relevant educational background
    • Degree related to the chosen specialization or study track
  3. Professional experience
    • Relevant work experience in water, sustainability, environment, or related sectors
  4. Motivation
    • Strong personal statement demonstrating commitment and future impact
  5. Recommendations
    • Strong academic and/or professional references

Remaining Costs After Scholarship

Students awarded the scholarship will still be responsible for the following estimated expenses:

  • Tuition Fee: €12,523 (including 21% VAT)
  • Insurance: €684 (subject to increase)
  • Visa Costs: €254 (subject to increase)
  • Living and Accommodation Costs: Approximately €14,640 for one year in the Netherlands

Applicants must provide proof that they can finance these remaining costs before they become eligible for final scholarship selection.

Delft Blue Top Water Scientist Scholarship

Scholarship Value

The Delft Blue Top Water Scientist Scholarship offers:

  • 50% reduction of tuition fees

This scholarship is specifically designed for students admitted to the Research MSc Programme in Water and Sustainable Development.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 1 July 2026

Selection Criteria

Selection may be based on:

  1. Outstanding academic achievements
  2. Relevant Bachelor’s or Master’s specialization
  3. Relevant research experience
  4. Relevant professional experience
  5. Strong motivation
  6. Quality and relevance of proposed research topic
  7. Excellent recommendation letters

Remaining Costs After Scholarship

Awardees will still need to cover:

  • Tuition Fee: €22,083 (including 21% VAT)
  • Insurance: €1,283 (subject to increase)
  • Visa Costs: €254 (subject to increase)
  • Living and Accommodation Costs: Approximately €27,450 for 22 months in the Netherlands

As with all Delft Blue scholarships, applicants must prove they have sufficient resources to cover these remaining costs.

Delft Blue Scholarship

Scholarship Value

The regular Delft Blue Scholarship provides:

  • 35% reduction of tuition fees

This scholarship is available for both:

  • MSc Programme in Water and Sustainable Development
  • Research MSc Programme in Water and Sustainable Development

Application Deadline

There is no fixed deadline for this scholarship.

Applications are reviewed on a:

First-come, first-served rolling basis

Interested applicants are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible because available scholarship slots may be filled before the academic year begins.

Remaining Costs for MSc Programme in Water and Sustainable Development

Recipients will be expected to cover:

  • Tuition Fee: €16,281 (including 21% VAT)
  • Insurance: €684
  • Visa Costs: €254
  • Living and Accommodation Costs: Approximately €14,640 for one year

Remaining Costs for Research MSc Programme in Water and Sustainable Development

Recipients will be expected to cover:

  • Tuition Fee: €28,707 (including 21% VAT)
  • Insurance: €1,283
  • Visa Costs: €254
  • Living and Accommodation Costs: Approximately €27,450 for 22 months

Important Scholarship Conditions

Applicants should carefully review the following conditions before applying:

1. Proof of Financial Capacity Is Required

All applicants must demonstrate that they have sufficient financial resources to cover the remaining tuition and living expenses after the scholarship reduction.

2. Scholarship Combination Restrictions

The regular Delft Blue Scholarship cannot be combined with the Water Leader Scholarship.

3. Other Scholarship Awards

If a recipient later receives another scholarship, such as:

  • Orange Knowledge Programme (OKP)
  • Rotary Scholarships
  • Other external funding opportunities

The already-paid tuition fee may be refunded, and the IHE Delft scholarship may be withdrawn.

4. Alternative Opportunity for Unsuccessful Applicants

Applicants who apply for the Water Leader Scholarship or Top Water Scientist Scholarship but are not selected may still apply for the regular Delft Blue Scholarship.

Why These Scholarships Matter

Water-related challenges continue to shape global development agendas, including:

  • Climate change adaptation
  • Sustainable water resource management
  • Safe drinking water access
  • Urban resilience
  • Coastal protection
  • Water governance
  • Environmental sustainability

The IHE Delft Blue Scholarships help ensure that talented students from around the world can access high-quality education and contribute to solving some of the world’s most pressing water challenges.

With tuition reductions of up to 50%, these scholarships significantly lower study costs while allowing students to gain internationally recognized expertise from one of the world’s leading water education institutions.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should first secure admission to one of the eligible master’s programmes at IHE Delft.

After receiving academic admission, eligible applicants can proceed with the scholarship application process by selecting the scholarship category that best matches their academic profile and programme of study.

Prospective applicants are encouraged to begin their admission and scholarship applications as early as possible, particularly those targeting the highly competitive Water Leader and Top Water Scientist Scholarships with the 1 July 2026 deadline.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post IHE Delft Blue Scholarships 2026–2028: Up to 50% Tuition Fee Reduction for International Master’s Students in Water and Sustainable Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ISDC Junior Researcher Position 2026 in Germany: Apply for a Fully Paid Research Opportunity in Berlin
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
International Security and Development Center (ISDC) Opens Applications for Junior Researcher Position in Berlin, Germany The International Security and Development Center (ISDC), a renowned non-profit academic research institute based in Berlin, Germany, has announced applications for a Junior Researcher (f/m/d) position within its Welfare Research Program. This exciting opportunity is ideal for talented early-career researchers, […]

The post ISDC Junior Researcher Position 2026 in Germany: Apply for a Fully Paid Research Opportunity in Berlin appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
International Security and Development Center (ISDC) Opens Applications for Junior Researcher Position in Berlin, Germany

The International Security and Development Center (ISDC), a renowned non-profit academic research institute based in Berlin, Germany, has announced applications for a Junior Researcher (f/m/d) position within its Welfare Research Program. This exciting opportunity is ideal for talented early-career researchers, economists, social scientists, and development professionals who are passionate about using evidence-based research to improve lives affected by conflict, fragility, and humanitarian emergencies.

The full-time position offers an opportunity to work with leading researchers and international partners on projects addressing some of the world’s most pressing humanitarian and development challenges. Successful candidates will join ISDC’s growing international team and contribute to cutting-edge research focused on resilience, poverty reduction, food security, and humanitarian interventions in fragile settings.

The position is expected to commence on 1 August 2026 and is based in Berlin, Germany.

About the International Security and Development Center (ISDC)

The International Security and Development Center (ISDC) is a non-profit academic institute dedicated to producing high-quality research that improves the lives and livelihoods of people affected by violent conflict, humanitarian crises, and fragile environments.

ISDC believes in the power of data, evidence, and rigorous analysis to understand complex social and economic challenges and develop practical solutions that contribute to sustainable development and human well-being.

Through collaboration with governments, international organizations, humanitarian agencies, donors, and academic institutions, ISDC generates research that informs policy decisions and strengthens interventions in vulnerable communities around the world.

About the Welfare Research Program

The Junior Researcher will be part of ISDC’s Welfare Research Program, which examines the relationship between humanitarian emergencies, conflict, fragility, and economic and social well-being.

The programme focuses on understanding how households and communities cope with shocks such as:

  • Armed conflict
  • Forced displacement
  • Economic crises
  • Food insecurity
  • Climate-related challenges
  • Humanitarian emergencies

Researchers use experimental and quasi-experimental methods to study household resilience and evaluate the effectiveness of humanitarian and development interventions.

The findings contribute to improving humanitarian assistance, poverty alleviation efforts, resilience-building programmes, and food and nutrition security initiatives.

Project Focus: Measuring Resilience in Syria

A major component of the role will involve supporting a project that evaluates the resilience impacts of a multi-sectoral humanitarian programme in Syria.

This important research seeks to understand how humanitarian interventions affect vulnerable households and how programmes can better support communities facing prolonged conflict and instability.

The selected candidate will contribute to generating evidence that may influence future humanitarian and development programming in conflict-affected regions.

Key Responsibilities

The Junior Researcher will play an important role in supporting project implementation, data management, analysis, and reporting activities.

Responsibilities include:

1. Survey Coordination

  • Support the coordination of quantitative micro-level survey data collection
  • Work closely with multiple implementing partners
  • Ensure efficient data collection processes

2. Data Cleaning and Management

  • Clean and organize large quantitative datasets
  • Match and verify survey data
  • Ensure high standards of data quality and consistency

3. Quantitative Analysis

  • Conduct statistical analyses using R
  • Generate insights from micro-level household and community data
  • Support evidence-based decision-making

4. Literature Reviews

  • Conduct comprehensive reviews of academic and policy literature
  • Summarize relevant findings
  • Support research design and interpretation

5. Research Writing

  • Contribute to research reports
  • Assist in drafting academic papers
  • Support dissemination of findings to partners and stakeholders

Required Qualifications

ISDC is seeking highly motivated candidates with strong quantitative research skills and a commitment to addressing global development challenges.

Applicants should possess:

Educational Background

  • An excellent Master’s degree in Economics or Social Sciences

Technical Skills

  • Strong expertise in statistical analysis using R
  • Advanced data management capabilities
  • Experience handling large quantitative datasets
  • Experience designing and managing surveys using ODK

Communication Skills

  • Excellent written English
  • Excellent oral communication skills in English

Additional Advantages

While not mandatory, the following are considered assets:

  • Experience working in fragile and conflict-affected settings
  • Knowledge of Arabic
  • Knowledge of German

Benefits of the Position

The ISDC Junior Researcher position offers an attractive package designed to support professional growth and work-life balance.

Competitive Salary

Successful candidates will receive:

  • Gross monthly salary of €3,341

International Research Exposure

Researchers will work with:

  • Leading academic experts
  • International organizations
  • Development agencies
  • Humanitarian actors
  • Global research networks

Professional Development

Employees gain:

  • Hands-on research experience
  • Exposure to high-impact projects
  • Opportunities for publication
  • Advanced quantitative research skills

Flexible Work Environment

The position includes:

  • Flexible working arrangements
  • Opportunity to work abroad for up to three weeks annually

Annual Leave

Employees receive:

  • 30 days of annual leave
  • Additional leave on December 24 and December 31

Collaborative Work Culture

Researchers will work within a diverse, international, and collaborative team located in central Berlin.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit a complete application package that includes:

  1. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  2. Cover Letter (Maximum One Page)

The cover letter should explain:

  • Why you are interested in the position
  • How your skills align with the role
  • Why you would be a strong fit for ISDC
  1. Academic Records
  • Bachelor’s degree certificate and transcript
  • Master’s degree certificate and transcript
  1. References

Either:

  • Two academic or professional recommendation letters

OR

  • Contact information for two references
