Please note that incomplete applications will not be considered.

Why You Should Apply

This position provides a rare opportunity for early-career researchers to contribute to impactful research that directly informs humanitarian and development policies in some of the world’s most vulnerable settings.

Candidates interested in:

  • Development economics
  • Humanitarian research
  • Conflict studies
  • Food security
  • Poverty reduction
  • Impact evaluation
  • Quantitative research

will find this role particularly valuable for building a meaningful international research career.

Application Deadline

The final deadline for applications is:

5 July 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible because interviews may be conducted on a rolling basis before the deadline.

Apply Here 

Final Thoughts

The ISDC Junior Researcher Position 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity to work at the intersection of research, humanitarian action, and development policy. With a competitive salary, international exposure, flexible work arrangements, and the chance to contribute to evidence-based solutions for conflict-affected populations, this role represents an excellent career step for aspiring researchers seeking to make a global impact.

For more information on this job, click here .

Discover more job opportunities on OFY, click here

 

The post ISDC Junior Researcher Position 2026 in Germany: Apply for a Fully Paid Research Opportunity in Berlin appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Stanford Biology Preview Program 2026: Fully Virtual Opportunity for Future PhD Students to Receive Mentorship, Application Guidance, and Graduate School Preparation
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
Students aspiring to pursue a PhD in Biology often face a challenging application process that requires strategic preparation, strong application materials, and a clear understanding of graduate school expectations. To help talented scholars navigate this journey, Stanford University’s Department of Biology has officially opened applications for the 2026 Stanford Biology Preview Program (BPP). This highly […]

The post Stanford Biology Preview Program 2026: Fully Virtual Opportunity for Future PhD Students to Receive Mentorship, Application Guidance, and Graduate School Preparation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Students aspiring to pursue a PhD in Biology often face a challenging application process that requires strategic preparation, strong application materials, and a clear understanding of graduate school expectations. To help talented scholars navigate this journey, Stanford University’s Department of Biology has officially opened applications for the 2026 Stanford Biology Preview Program (BPP).

This highly valuable virtual program is specifically designed to support prospective doctoral students from diverse and non-traditional backgrounds by providing mentorship, application guidance, workshops, networking opportunities, and direct interaction with Stanford faculty, graduate students, postdoctoral researchers, and staff.

Applications for the Stanford Biology Preview Program 2026 opened on May 11, 2026, and will close on July 19, 2026. The program itself will be conducted online on September 10–11, 2026, allowing participants from around the world to engage without the need for travel.

What is the Stanford Biology Preview Program (BPP)?

The Stanford Biology Preview Program is an initiative developed by Stanford University’s Department of Biology to help prospective PhD applicants better understand the graduate admissions process.

The program is not a degree program and participation does not guarantee admission into Stanford’s Biology PhD Program. Instead, it serves as a preparatory experience that helps applicants strengthen their understanding of doctoral applications and become more competitive candidates.

Through a series of virtual workshops and mentoring sessions, participants gain insights into:

  • The Stanford Biology PhD application process
  • Graduate school expectations
  • Research opportunities
  • Preparing competitive application materials
  • Selecting appropriate research tracks
  • Building relationships with faculty and graduate students

The initiative is particularly focused on expanding access and ensuring that talented students from diverse backgrounds have the information and support necessary to pursue advanced scientific training.

Why Stanford Created the Biology Preview Program

Stanford recognizes that the future of biological research depends on attracting talented scientists with varied perspectives, experiences, and backgrounds.

Research consistently demonstrates that diverse teams are more innovative and effective at solving complex scientific problems. However, many highly capable students may have limited exposure to graduate admissions processes or lack access to mentoring networks that can guide them through PhD applications.

The Biology Preview Program was created to address these barriers by:

  1. Increasing awareness of Stanford’s Biology PhD Program.
  2. Demystifying the graduate admissions process.
  3. Providing personalized mentorship.
  4. Encouraging talented students from diverse backgrounds to pursue doctoral education.
  5. Strengthening future applications through guidance and feedback.

Program Highlights

Participants selected for the 2026 cohort will benefit from a comprehensive virtual experience that includes:

1. Interactive Workshops

Attendees will participate in specialized workshops focused on:

  • Graduate school admissions
  • Application preparation
  • Personal statement development
  • Research experience presentation
  • Building strong academic profiles
  • Understanding doctoral training

2. Individualized Mentorship

One of the program’s most valuable components is personalized mentorship.

Participants will receive:

  • Feedback on application materials
  • Advice from current PhD students
  • Guidance from faculty members
  • Insights into academic career pathways

3. Networking Opportunities

The program provides direct engagement with:

  • Stanford Biology faculty
  • Current PhD students
  • Postdoctoral scholars
  • Administrative staff

These interactions help participants gain realistic perspectives on graduate education and research careers.

4. Exposure to Stanford’s Biology PhD Tracks

Participants will learn about Stanford’s two primary Biology PhD pathways:

Cellular, Molecular, and Organismal Biology (CMOB)

This track focuses on:

  • Cell biology
  • Molecular biology
  • Genetics
  • Development
  • Physiology
  • Organismal biology

Ecology and Evolution (Eco/Evo)

This track focuses on:

  • Evolutionary biology
  • Ecology
  • Biodiversity
  • Population genetics
  • Species interactions
  • Environmental biology

The program helps applicants understand differences between these tracks and determine which aligns best with their research interests.

Benefits of Participating in the Stanford Biology Preview Program

Selected participants can expect numerous benefits, including:

Academic Benefits

  • Better understanding of PhD admissions
  • Enhanced graduate school readiness
  • Improved application materials
  • Exposure to cutting-edge biological research

Professional Benefits

  • Networking with Stanford researchers
  • Mentorship from current PhD students
  • Access to scientific career insights
  • Development of professional relationships

Personal Development Benefits

  • Increased confidence in applying to graduate school
  • Improved understanding of academic expectations
  • Stronger preparation for doctoral studies

Important Program Dates

Application Opens

May 11, 2026

Application Deadline

July 19, 2026

Program Dates

September 10–11, 2026

Program Format

Fully Online

Eligibility Criteria

The Stanford Biology Preview Program welcomes applicants from diverse and non-traditional backgrounds.

The organizers particularly encourage applications from individuals who can contribute to diversity in STEM disciplines.

Potential applicants may include:

  • First-generation college students
  • Low-income students
  • Students from historically underrepresented backgrounds
  • International students
  • Non-traditional learners
  • Individuals with unique life experiences and perspectives

Applicants should demonstrate a genuine interest in pursuing doctoral studies in biology-related fields.

What Participants Will Learn

Throughout the program, participants will gain knowledge on:

  • Graduate admissions strategies
  • Research statement preparation
  • Personal statement development
  • Identifying faculty mentors
  • Building strong applications
  • Understanding Stanford’s academic culture
  • Navigating doctoral training pathways

Important Note

Participation in the Stanford Biology Preview Program:

  • Does not guarantee admission to Stanford.
  • Is not required to apply to Stanford’s Biology PhD Program.
  • Is intended solely as a preparatory and mentoring opportunity.

The program is designed to help prospective applicants make informed decisions and submit stronger graduate applications.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their applications before the deadline and review the official program information available through Stanford University’s Department of Biology.

For questions regarding the program, applicants may contact the organizing committee via:

stanfordbiologypreviewprogram@gmail.com

Apply Here 

For more information on this program, visit here

To discover more global opportunities on OFY, click here 

The post Stanford Biology Preview Program 2026: Fully Virtual Opportunity for Future PhD Students to Receive Mentorship, Application Guidance, and Graduate School Preparation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Ellison Scholars Programme 2026: Fully Funded Oxford University Scholarships for Global Changemakers
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Apply for the Prestigious Ellison Scholars Programme 2026 and Study at the University of Oxford with Full Funding Applications are now open for the highly competitive Ellison Scholars Programme 2026, a transformative opportunity that offers fully funded undergraduate and graduate study at the University of Oxford while empowering students to work on innovative solutions to […]

The post Ellison Scholars Programme 2026: Fully Funded Oxford University Scholarships for Global Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Apply for the Prestigious Ellison Scholars Programme 2026 and Study at the University of Oxford with Full Funding

Applications are now open for the highly competitive Ellison Scholars Programme 2026, a transformative opportunity that offers fully funded undergraduate and graduate study at the University of Oxford while empowering students to work on innovative solutions to some of humanity’s greatest challenges.

Supported by the Ellison Institute of Technology (EIT), this unique scholarship programme goes far beyond traditional financial aid. It combines world-class academic training, cutting-edge research, project-based learning, mentorship from global experts, paid internship opportunities, and direct engagement with breakthrough technologies designed to improve the future of humanity.

The programme seeks exceptional students from around the world who demonstrate academic excellence, leadership potential, intellectual curiosity, and a strong commitment to addressing global challenges through science, technology, innovation, and interdisciplinary collaboration.

About the Ellison Scholars Programme

The Ellison Institute of Technology (EIT) was established with a bold vision: to accelerate technological innovation and scientific discovery that can solve the world’s most pressing and enduring problems.

Through its Education & Scholarships Programme, EIT invests in the next generation of innovators, researchers, entrepreneurs, and leaders who are passionate about creating meaningful impact on a global scale.

Successful scholars become part of a vibrant international community where academic excellence is combined with real-world problem-solving and innovation.

Unlike many conventional scholarship programmes, Ellison Scholars are not only funded to study at one of the world’s leading universities; they are also actively involved in interdisciplinary projects that address critical global issues.

What the Scholarship Covers

The Ellison Scholars Programme provides a comprehensive funding package designed to remove financial barriers and allow students to focus on learning, research, and innovation.

Benefits include:

1. Fully Funded Study at the University of Oxford

Selected scholars will receive funding to pursue undergraduate or doctoral studies at the University of Oxford, one of the world’s most prestigious academic institutions.

2. Generous Living Stipend

Recipients receive financial support for living expenses throughout their studies, ensuring they can fully engage in academic and research activities.

3. Paid Internships

Undergraduate scholars benefit from paid internship opportunities, allowing them to gain practical experience while contributing to impactful projects.

4. Project-Based Learning

Students participate in real-world innovation projects that tackle global challenges and create meaningful societal impact.

5. Access to World-Class Experts

Scholars work alongside internationally renowned scientists, innovators, researchers, and technology leaders.

6. State-of-the-Art Facilities

Participants gain access to advanced laboratories, research infrastructure, and cutting-edge technological resources at EIT and Oxford.

7. Global Network and Community

Students become part of a growing network of scholars, researchers, innovators, and global change-makers dedicated to solving humanity’s most pressing problems.

Humane Endeavours: Areas of Innovation

A distinctive feature of the Ellison Scholars Programme is its focus on what EIT refers to as “Humane Endeavours” — major areas of scientific and technological innovation aimed at improving human wellbeing and sustainability.

Scholars collaborate on projects related to:

Health, Medical Science and Generative Biology

This area focuses on advancing healthcare, improving disease prevention and treatment, and leveraging biotechnology to enhance human health.

Food Security and Sustainable Agriculture

Projects seek innovative solutions that improve agricultural productivity, strengthen food systems, and promote sustainability.

Climate Change and Atmospheric Carbon Management

Researchers and innovators work on strategies and technologies to address climate change and reduce atmospheric carbon dioxide levels.

Artificial Intelligence and Robotics

Students contribute to the development of advanced AI systems and robotic technologies capable of addressing complex global challenges.

Undergraduate Scholarship Opportunities

The undergraduate pathway enables talented students to pursue a fully funded bachelor’s degree at the University of Oxford while participating in EIT’s interdisciplinary innovation ecosystem.

Students gain:

  • Full financial support
  • Access to Oxford’s academic resources
  • Paid internships
  • Mentorship from industry and research leaders
  • Hands-on project experience
  • Exposure to breakthrough technologies

This pathway is ideal for ambitious young people who want to combine academic excellence with practical impact.

Graduate and Doctoral Opportunities

Graduate scholars undertake doctoral-level research while working closely with EIT teams on ambitious scientific and technological projects.

The doctoral pathway offers:

  • Full funding for study and research
  • Access to leading researchers and facilities
  • Participation in multidisciplinary innovation initiatives
  • Opportunities to contribute directly to global problem-solving efforts

Researchers work year-round alongside EIT experts, translating academic discoveries into practical solutions with real-world impact.

Life at EIT Oxford

EIT Oxford is rapidly emerging as a world-leading hub for research, innovation, and technological advancement.

Students benefit from:

  • Collaborative research environments
  • Access to cutting-edge technologies
  • Interdisciplinary teamwork
  • Mentorship from global experts
  • Innovation-driven learning experiences
  • Strong professional networks

The programme fosters a culture where curiosity, creativity, and collaboration are encouraged to drive meaningful change.

Why You Should Apply

The Ellison Scholars Programme is ideal for students who:

  • Want to study at Oxford University
  • Aspire to become future scientific and technological leaders
  • Are passionate about solving global challenges
  • Seek hands-on research and innovation experience
  • Want access to world-class mentors and facilities
  • Desire a fully funded educational opportunity

The programme provides a rare combination of academic excellence, financial support, professional development, and global impact.

Who Should Consider Applying?

Strong candidates include:

  • Future scientists
  • Engineers
  • Researchers
  • Innovators
  • Technology enthusiasts
  • Sustainability advocates
  • Future entrepreneurs
  • Leaders interested in social impact

Applicants should demonstrate a strong desire to use knowledge and innovation to address real-world challenges.

Application Process

Prospective applicants should:

  1. Review eligibility requirements.
  2. Select their preferred programme pathway (Undergraduate or Graduate).
  3. Prepare application materials.
  4. Complete the online application through the official Ellison Scholars platform.
  5. Demonstrate academic excellence, leadership potential, and commitment to solving global problems.

Apply Here 

Final Thoughts

The Ellison Scholars Programme 2026 represents one of the most exciting fully funded educational opportunities available to students worldwide. By combining Oxford University education with advanced research, innovation, mentorship, and real-world impact, the programme equips future leaders with the tools needed to solve humanity’s most pressing challenges.

Students passionate about science, technology, sustainability, healthcare, food security, climate solutions, artificial intelligence, and global development are strongly encouraged to explore this life-changing opportunity and begin their application journey.

For more information on this scholarship opportunity, click here 

Discover more scholarship opportunities on OFY, here.

The post Ellison Scholars Programme 2026: Fully Funded Oxford University Scholarships for Global Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
